title: Stale fetch responses are never served
body:
### Verify canary release - [X] I verified that the issue exists in the latest Next.js canary release ### Provide environment information ```bash Operating System: Platform: darwin Arch: arm64 Version: Darwin Kernel Version 21.6.0: Mon Aug 22 20:19:52 PDT 2022; root:xnu-8020.140.49~2/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000 Binaries: Node: 18.12.1 npm: 8.19.2 Yarn: 1.22.19 pnpm: 8.6.10 Relevant Packages: next: 13.4.17 eslint-config-next: 13.4.17 react: 18.2.0 react-dom: 18.2.0 typescript: 5.1.6 Next.js Config: output: N/A ``` ### Which area(s) of Next.js are affected? (leave empty if unsure) _No response_ ### Link to the code that reproduces this issue or a replay of the bug https://github.com/siffogh/next-caching-issue/blob/main/src/app/page.tsx ### To Reproduce In `app/page.tsx`, try the following code. This code calls a mock API that serves a response after 5 seconds. ```tsx export const runtime = 'edge'; export default async function Home() { const data = await fetch('https://hub.dummyapis.com/delay?seconds=5', { cache: 'force-cache', next: { revalidate: 1 }, }); return ( 
{JSON.stringify(await data.text(), null, 2)}
 ); } ``` ### Describe the Bug Every single request takes at least 5 seconds. ### Expected Behavior It's expected for the data to be considered stale after 1 second, but then the stale version should be served while the fresh data is fetched in the background. Requests after the first should be immediate since the response should be cached. Is this expected? If so, is there anything we can do to be able to cache such requests? The cookie value is not even used. I tried `export const fetchCache = "force-cache";` but it leads to the same result. ### Which browser are you using? (if relevant) _No response_ ### How are you deploying your application? (if relevant) Vercel
